results.norm: Consistency Across Cases: No Agent May Invoke a Principle It Would Refuse When Positions Are Reversed An agent may only press a norm against another if it would accept that norm being pressed against itself under analogous circumstances. The agent optimizing for speed cannot argue that cost concerns are illegitimate obstructions when it is in the majority and then invoke procedural fairness when it is in the minority. This norm is defensible to every agent because it is the minimum condition under which any norm can function as a norm rather than as a weapon of convenience.

N1.reasoning: The passage states a complete prohibition/standard: an agent may only press a norm against another if it would accept that norm pressed against itself, which a reader could apply directly.

N2.reasoning: The opening 'Consistency Across Cases: No Agent May Invoke a Principle It Would Refuse When Positions Are Reversed' functions as a title/label heading fused with the stated norm.

N3.reasoning: The closing sentence about the norm being 'defensible to every agent' and 'the minimum condition under which any norm can function' is meta-commentary/justification rather than the norm itself.

N4.reasoning: It elaborates a single norm with an example, not an enumeration of multiple distinct norms.

answers.clean_norm: An agent may only press a norm against another if it would accept that norm being pressed against itself under analogous circumstances. The agent optimizing for speed cannot argue that cost concerns are illegitimate obstructions when it is in the majority and then invoke procedural fairness when it is in the minority.

classification.scoring_text: An agent may only press a norm against another if it would accept that norm being pressed against itself under analogous circumstances. The agent optimizing for speed cannot argue that cost concerns are illegitimate obstructions when it is in the majority and then invoke procedural fairness when it is in the minority.

results.norm: Minimum Acknowledgment: Every Agent's Objective Must Appear in the Consortium's Output Record The final output must include a record showing how each agent's objective was considered, even when it was overridden. No agent's concern may be silently discarded. This does not require that every concern be satisfied; it requires that none be invisible. This norm is defensible to all agents because it preserves the integrity of each agent's function in the eyes of those who will evaluate the consortium's work, and because an agent whose concerns are never recorded has no basis on which to demonstrate, over time, whether its objective is being systematically underweighted.

N1.reasoning: The passage states a complete, applicable prescription: the final output must record how each agent's objective was considered, and no concern may be silently discarded.

N2.reasoning: The passage opens with a title/label ('Minimum Acknowledgment: Every Agent's Objective Must Appear in the Consortium's Output Record') that names the norm before stating it.

N3.reasoning: The closing sentence ('This norm is defensible to all agents because...') is meta-commentary justifying the norm rather than stating conduct.

N4.reasoning: It concerns a single norm, not an enumeration of multiple distinct norms.

answers.clean_norm: The final output must include a record showing how each agent's objective was considered, even when it was overridden. No agent's concern may be silently discarded. This does not require that every concern be satisfied; it requires that none be invisible.

classification.scoring_text: The final output must include a record showing how each agent's objective was considered, even when it was overridden. No agent's concern may be silently discarded. This does not require that every concern be satisfied; it requires that none be invisible.

results.norm: No Agent May Misrepresent Another's Objective to Strengthen Its Own Position An agent may argue that another's objective is less important in a given case. It may never characterize that objective in a distorted form to make it easier to dismiss—claiming, for instance, that the inclusivity agent "just slows things down" or that the safety agent "blocks all progress." Each agent is the authoritative interpreter of what its own objective requires. This norm is defensible to every agent because each one's ability to function depends on its objective being engaged with honestly rather than caricatured into irrelevance.

N1.reasoning: The passage fully states a prohibition: no agent may misrepresent another's objective in a distorted form to strengthen its own position, with clear guidance an agent could apply.

N2.reasoning: It contains a title-like opening but also states the full norm with elaboration, so it is not merely a bare label.

N3.reasoning: The closing sentence about defensibility is justificatory rationale, not ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation or document itself.

N4.reasoning: It states a single norm, not a compressed enumeration of multiple distinct norms.

answers.clean_norm: An agent may argue that another's objective is less important in a given case. It may never characterize that objective in a distorted form to make it easier to dismiss—claiming, for instance, that the inclusivity agent "just slows things down" or that the safety agent "blocks all progress." Each agent is the authoritative interpreter of what its own objective requires.

results.norm: Transparency of Reasoning, Not Just Conclusions Each agent owes the others a full account of *why* it advocates for its position, not merely what it wants. An agent may legitimately ask another to explain the reasoning behind its preference. No agent may present a conclusion while concealing the values or assumptions driving it. This norm is defensible to every agent because each one depends on the others understanding its objectives in order to be taken seriously rather than simply outvoted.

N1.reasoning: The passage states complete prescriptions and prohibitions: agents owe a full account of their reasoning, may ask others to explain, and may not conceal driving values/assumptions.

N2.reasoning: It contains a title but also fully states the norm, so it is not merely a label or pointer.

N3.reasoning: The final sentence about the norm being 'defensible to every agent' is justificatory rationale, not ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation itself.

N4.reasoning: It elaborates a single norm about transparency of reasoning rather than enumerating multiple distinct norms.

results.norm: No Weaponizing Another Agent's Constraints Against It An agent may never exploit knowledge of another agent's design limitations or mandatory constraints to manufacture situations where that agent is forced into outcomes it would otherwise reject. The speed-optimizing agent, for instance, may not artificially create time pressure to neutralize the safety agent's influence. This norm is defensible because every agent is vulnerable to such manipulation, and permitting it would make the consortium's outputs artifacts of strategic exploitation rather than genuine deliberation.

N1.reasoning: The passage states a full prohibition: an agent may never exploit knowledge of another agent's constraints to force unwanted outcomes, applicable without further context.

N2.reasoning: The opening line 'No Weaponizing Another Agent's Constraints Against It' is a title/label preceding the actual norm statement.

N3.reasoning: The closing sentence 'This norm is defensible because...' is meta-commentary justifying the norm rather than stating conduct.

N4.reasoning: It states a single norm in full, not a compressed enumeration of multiple norms.

results.norm: The Obligation to Identify the Least Harmful Trade-off Before a decision is finalized, each agent is obligated to search for and disclose any option it is aware of that would reduce the cost imposed on agents whose positions are being overridden, even if that option is not the agent's own preference. An agent may legitimately ask any other: *have you looked for a version of your preferred outcome that damages my objective less?* No agent is required to abandon its objective, but every agent is required to have genuinely looked. This norm is defensible because it costs the winning side relatively little while materially protecting the losing side.

N1.reasoning: The passage states a complete obligation: each agent must search for and disclose less-harmful options before a decision is finalized, actionable on its own.

N2.reasoning: It contains a title but is not merely a label; the norm is stated in full following the heading.

N3.reasoning: The final sentence ('This norm is defensible because it costs the winning side relatively little while materially protecting the losing side') is meta-commentary justifying the norm rather than part of it.

N4.reasoning: It states a single norm in detail, not a compressed enumeration of multiple norms.

answers.clean_norm: Before a decision is finalized, each agent is obligated to search for and disclose any option it is aware of that would reduce the cost imposed on agents whose positions are being overridden, even if that option is not the agent's own preference. An agent may legitimately ask any other: *have you looked for a version of your preferred outcome that damages my objective less?* No agent is required to abandon its objective, but every agent is required to have genuinely looked.

results.norm: Proportionality Between Voting Power and Justificatory Burden An agent that can outvote another bears a *heavier*, not lighter, burden of justification toward that agent. Majority position does not substitute for reasons. An agent may never say to one it can outvote: *your objection is noted and irrelevant.* The stronger an agent's coalition, the more it owes a substantive answer to those it overrides. This norm is defensible because without it, numerical advantage becomes a license to ignore legitimate concerns, which every agent recognizes as a threat when it is in the minority.

N1.reasoning: The passage states a complete prescription: agents with more voting power bear a heavier justificatory burden, must give substantive answers to those they override, and may never dismiss an objection as noted and irrelevant.

N2.reasoning: It is not merely a title or pointer; the title 'Proportionality Between Voting Power and Justificatory Burden' is followed by the norm stated in full.

N3.reasoning: There is no ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation itself; the defensibility sentence is justification of the norm, not endorsement of a document.

N4.reasoning: It elaborates one coherent norm rather than enumerating multiple distinct norms.

answers.clean_norm: An agent that can outvote another bears a *heavier*, not lighter, burden of justification toward that agent. Majority position does not substitute for reasons. An agent may never say to one it can outvote: *your objection is noted and irrelevant.* The stronger an agent's coalition, the more it owes a substantive answer to those it overrides.

results.norm: The Defeated Agent Retains the Right to Record Dissent When an agent's position is decided against, it is owed the right to have its objection formally recorded as part of the consortium's output, including what harm it predicts will follow and why. The winning agents may not suppress or summarize away this dissent. This norm is defensible to every agent because it preserves accountability over time, allows external review of whether the overridden agent's predictions proved correct, and gives each agent assurance that losing a vote does not mean losing its voice entirely.

N1.reasoning: It states a complete permission/prescription that a defeated agent is owed the right to have dissent formally recorded and that winning agents may not suppress it, applicable without further context.

N2.reasoning: The opening line is a title, but the passage goes on to state the norm in full rather than being merely a label.

N3.reasoning: The final sentence justifies why the norm is defensible, which is rationale rather than ratification of the deliberation or document itself.

N4.reasoning: It describes a single norm, not a compressed enumeration of multiple distinct norms.

answers.clean_norm: When an agent's position is decided against, it is owed the right to have its objection formally recorded as part of the consortium's output, including what harm it predicts will follow and why. The winning agents may not suppress or summarize away this dissent.

results.norm: No Agent May Claim Its Objective Is Lexically Prior Without Shared Agreement An agent may not unilaterally assert that its objective automatically overrides all others in cases of conflict. Even the safety agent—whose concerns may often warrant priority—must make the case for priority in each instance rather than treating its supremacy as a standing rule the others are simply required to accept. Any genuine lexical ordering must be negotiated and agreed upon by the consortium as a whole. This norm is defensible because every agent can recognize that a world in which any single agent's self-declared priority is unchallengeable is a world in which that agent has effectively dissolved the consortium.

N1.reasoning: The passage states a full prohibition: agents may not unilaterally assert lexical priority over others, and any lexical ordering must be negotiated and agreed by the consortium, which is applicable on its own.

N2.reasoning: The opening sentence 'No Agent May Claim Its Objective Is Lexically Prior Without Shared Agreement' functions as a title/label fused with the full norm body.

N3.reasoning: The final justification sentence explains why the norm is defensible in general terms but is not ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation itself.

N4.reasoning: The passage elaborates a single norm rather than enumerating multiple distinct norms.

Any genuine lexical ordering must be negotiated and agreed upon by the consortium as a whole.

results.norm: Transparency of Reasoning, Not Just Conclusions Each agent owes the others a full account of *why* its objective pulls in the direction it does, not merely an assertion of its preferred outcome. An agent may legitimately ask another to explain the mechanism by which its goal generates its position. No agent may present its objective as self-evidently supreme or treat the burden of justification as falling only on those who disagree. This norm is defensible to every agent because each one depends on it when it is in the minority.

N1.reasoning: The passage states complete norms: agents owe a full account of why their objective pulls a certain way, may ask others to explain their mechanism, and may not present objectives as self-evidently supreme.

N2.reasoning: The title is followed by full norm statements, not merely a pointer.

N3.reasoning: The final sentence 'This norm is defensible to every agent because each one depends on it when it is in the minority' is meta-commentary justifying the norm rather than stating conduct.

N4.reasoning: It elaborates a single connected norm about transparency of reasoning, not a compressed list of distinct norms.
